records.ety: [F. dsavouer; pref. ds- (L. dis-) + avouer to avow. See Avow, and cf. Disavouch.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disavowed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disavowing.]

defs: To refuse strongly and solemnly to own or acknowledge; to deny responsibility for, approbation of, and the like; to disclaim; to disown; as, he was charged with embezzlement, but he disavows the crime.
quotes: A solemn promise made and disavowed.

defs: To deny; to show the contrary of; to disprove.
quotes: Yet can they never Toss into air the freedom of my birth, Or disavow my blood Plantagenet's.
